title: Understanding the Position Function
text: The position function is given as $$r(t)=cos\left(1t\right) i+sin\left(1t\right) j+t k$$. This represents a helical path where the particle moves in a circle in the xy-plane while moving linearly along the $$z-axis$$.

title: Finding the Velocity Vector
text: To find the velocity vector, differentiate the position function with respect to time $$t$$. Remember that the derivative of $$cos(1.0t)$$ is $$-sin(1.0t)$$ and the derivative of $$sin(1.0t)$$ is $$cos(1.0t)$$.

title: Calculate the Velocity Vector
text: Differentiate $$r(t)=cos\left(1t\right) i+sin\left(1t\right) j+t k$$ with respect to $$t$$ to find the velocity vector v(t).

title: Finding the Acceleration Vector
text: To find the acceleration vector, differentiate the velocity vector with respect to time $$t$$. Remember that the derivative of $$-sin(1.0t)$$ is $$-cos(1.0t)$$ and the derivative of $$cos(1.0t)$$ is $$-sin(1.0t)$$.

title: Calculate the Acceleration Vector
text: Differentiate $$v(t)=-sin\left(1t\right) i+cos\left(1t\right) j+k$$ with respect to $$t$$ to find the acceleration vector a(t).
